digest.component_or_area: claude-skills shared test-design standard and skill wiring
propositions: Create a canonical shared/test-design.md TDD standard covering coverage inventory, contract card, tier-selection table, trigger/assert rule, oracle minimum, contrast case, fault realism, gating proof, defect-seeded mutation, and quantified LLM evals, with the scale-ceremony-to-the-bug principle stated.

Ship byte-identical real-file copies of test-design.md in each of the three skill directories (rebar-debug, rebar-implement, rebar-brainstorm), using same-directory companion files rather than symlinks so the standard resolves in every load context.
Add a gating unit test test_skill_shared_sync.py that asserts each per-skill copy is byte-identical to the canonical file, fails on divergence or absence, and runs under make test.

Wire rebar-debug SKILL.md to load the shared file: retitle Step 4 to RED-oracle design, defer its LLM-surface paragraph to the quantified-eval standard, make Step 5 mutation defect-seeded, and add the contract card as a fourth fixer handoff item without exposing held-out test code.
Update llm-behavioral-taxonomy.md's Phase 2 (Repair) bullet to cite test-design.md's quantified-eval requirements so it no longer defines RED as a single failing sample.

Integrate the shared file into rebar-implement Phase 4a/4b and rebar-brainstorm's ticket-recording gate, and document the canonical-file plus byte-identical-copies sync convention in README.md, with no user- or machine-specific paths anywhere.
